---
title: "Measure-to-measure interpolation using Transformers"
subtitle: "A machine-checked formalization and numerical validation"
toc: false
---

This site accompanies a Lean 4 formalization and numerical validation of

> M. Geshkovski, P. Rigollet, Y. Ruiz-Balet, *[Measure-to-measure interpolation using Transformers](https://arxiv.org/abs/2411.04551)*, arXiv:2411.04551v3.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Who did what.** The paper, its theorems, and its proofs are the work of **Geshkovski, Rigollet, and
Ruiz-Balet**. The **Lean 4 formalization, the numerical experiments, the prose informalizations, this
website, and the blueprint** are the work of **Alexandre Quemy**. Throughout, the original authors'
statements and proofs are reproduced verbatim in clearly marked, page-cited grey boxes, for scholarly
comparison; everything in blue or green boxes is the present author's contribution.
:::

## The result, in one paragraph

A Transformer can be viewed as the flow map of a continuity equation on the unit sphere
$\mathbb S^{d-1}$: each token is a particle, and a single time-dependent parameter
$\theta = (\mathbf V, \mathbf B, \mathbf W, \mathbf U, b)$ drives every particle through a
self-attention plus perceptron velocity field, projected to the tangent space by layer normalization.
The paper proves a **controllability** result: one such Transformer, with explicit
piecewise-constant parameters and a bounded number of switches, can steer $N$ arbitrary input measures
to $N$ arbitrary target measures, to within $\varepsilon$ in the Wasserstein distance $W_2$. The
construction is a three-act composition:

1. **Disentangle** (Prop 3.1) -- a piecewise-constant control separates the $N$ overlapping supports into pairwise-disjoint regions, using barycenter separation.
2. **Cluster** (Prop 2.1 / 2.2) -- self-attention contracts each measure to a point (or to $M$ atoms).
3. **Match** (Prop 4.1 / 4.2) -- a perceptron gate steers each cluster to its target while parking the others.

Theorem 1.1 handles Dirac targets sharing a missing direction; Theorem 1.2 handles
transport-matchable targets.

## What this project adds

- **Machine-checked leaves.** The self-contained computational cores of the proofs (the gate ODE, the barycenter ODE, the geodesic gradient, the separating hyperplane, the Lyapunov sign, the ball-chain retention, the pigeonhole step, and the disjoint-hulls/non-colinear-barycenters step) are isolated and given clean Lean 4 kernel proofs.
- **Appendix B mass transport, fully machine-checked.** Lemmas B.1 and B.2 are **proved theorems** whose kernel footprint is exactly `propext` / `Classical.choice` / `Quot.sound`: the ReLU-gated cutoff velocity field of B.5 as a genuine well-posed `Block`, the finite-time logistic reaching estimate (B.7), the pushforward mass-retention bridge (B.8), and the two-cap assembly via a recentring construction with an amplitude-scaled gate.
- **A labeled, fidelity-audited axiom layer.** The optimal-transport theory, the linear characteristic flow, and the measure-dependent mean-field attention dynamics (both existence **and** uniqueness of the McKean-Vlasov flow) are all built and machine-checked, so the mean-field layer carries zero axioms; the remaining paper-level results (LaSalle clustering, disentanglement, parking, $L^2$ density) are explicit named axioms — **9 in all**, every one a `Statements/`-layer paper claim rather than infrastructure — so every downstream result shows exactly what it rests on under `#print axioms`. Every axiom is admitted through an adversarial statement-fidelity protocol (verbatim source anchor, hypothesis-parity diff, refutation attempt) and guarded by a committed refutation regression suite.
- **Corrections and strengthenings.** A load-bearing sign error in the gate of Lemma B.2 (**F1**, see [errata](about.qmd)), caught by the formalization and an experiment; a rigor gap in Proposition 3.1's induction (**F2**) discharged by a machine-checked leaf; and a statement-fidelity audit (**F11--F21**) that kernel-refuted and repaired the loosely-transcribed axiom stubs -- two of them, `lemma_5_1` (**F19**, a `Measure.map` measurability escape) and `meanFieldFlow_unique` (**F20**, unsound off the sphere), found unsound and then *discharged* to machine-checked theorems -- surfacing three erratum candidates in the paper (**E2**, **E3**, **E4**). The mid-level **Lemma 3.4, Part 1** (App. B.3's non-colinearity result on the *linear* layer) was *discharged* from a labeled axiom to a machine-checked theorem via a mass-gap cap-collapse (axiom count 11 → 10), and mean-field **existence** (`exists_meanFieldFlow`) followed the same leaf-decomposition template as its uniqueness sibling (10 → **9**, the current count).
- **A seeded validation campaign.** Seven experiments integrate the actual dynamics and confirm the quantitative content of the claims, with figures and provenance manifests.

## Explore

- [**Blueprint**](proofs.qmd) -- every node: the original statement and proof, our Lean formalization, and what it strengthens. ([standalone PDF](blueprint.pdf))
- [**Experiments**](experiments.qmd) -- the seven seeded validation experiments, with hypotheses, figures, and verdicts.
- [**Claim graph**](claimgraph.qmd) -- the interactive dependency graph and its commit-by-commit history.
- [**About**](about.qmd) -- citation, attribution, errata, and licensing.
